metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes (V2 TUI queues mid-turn messages)
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

**agent (yes).** kiro-cli's V2 TUI explicitly queues a follow-up message typed while the model is working â€” it is NOT dropped or sent as literal text. The kiro-cli 2.5.1 binary's embedded changelog carries two decisive entries: `"Added UX for managing queued messages in V2 TUI"` and `"Reject queued ! shell-escape commands during processing instead of sending them as literal text"`. The second confirms that messages typed *during processing* are queued (the special-case is only the `!` shell-escape, which is rejected) â€” so a plain follow-up is queued and consumed at the next safe point, exactly like Claude Code. This resolves the question the dispatch flagged as unverified (some TUIs drop or block mid-turn input): kiro V2 queues.

**daemon (full).** The spec asserts three observables: (1) state stays `working` through the queued-message arrival, (2) no `â†’ ready` flicker when the queued input lands, (3) the cycle still ends at `ready`. irrlicht's kirocli parser drives state off transcript-line shape, not off keystrokes. The queued text is buffered inside the kiro process and is NOT appended to `~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl` until the current turn ends â€” so the daemon's transcript order is `Prompt(A) â€¦ turn_done, Prompt(B) â€¦`. Throughout turn A the trailing line is an `AssistantMessage` carrying `toolUse` blocks (parsed `assistant`) or a `ToolResults` line (parsed `tool_result`) â€” neither is `turn_done`, so the session stays `working` with no flicker to `ready`. Only the FINAL text-only `AssistantMessage` of turn A maps to `turn_done` (parser.go lines 71-79, confirmed by parser_test.go's multi-tool sequence ending in a text-only `turn_done`). The queued `Prompt(B)` then drives a fresh `working` span that ends at `ready`. All three observables are produced.

**driver (ready).** The recipe needs `send` (the mid-turn follow-up, with NO preceding `wait_turn` so it lands while turn A is still in flight), `wait_turn`, and `sleep`. All three are in the driver's `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"`. kiro's `send_text` uses `tmux send-keys -l -- "$text"` then `Enter` â€” the same keystroke mechanism the claudecode reference recipe expressed via its `keys` step â€” so a queued mid-turn send is just a `send`; no `keys` primitive is required and there is no driver gap.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

The queue itself is invisible â€” there is no event for "user typed text that is now buffered." The spec asserts no such event; it asserts only state invariants on the working span, which the daemon verifies from its event stream. The kiro JSONL also carries NO model/token/cost (those live in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar the v1 parser does not read), so the observation vector is `model: unknown`, `cost_nonzero: false`, `tokens_nonzero: false`.
caveats: PROVISIONAL on emission ordering: that the queued Prompt(B) line is appended to the JSONL only AFTER turn A's text-only turn_done (never interleaved mid-turn as a synthetic user-line that the parser would read as a boundary) is inferred from the smoke FINDINGS turn-shape and parser.go, not from a live mid-turn-queue recording. If kiro ever wrote the queued Prompt before consumption, the parser could briefly flip toward a boundary â€” exactly the regression these invariants guard. record settles this from provisional to confirmed.

Mid-turn send is a single `send` with NO preceding `wait_turn` (unlike the consume-then-send sequence): the second prompt must land while turn A is still working. The driver's send_text bumps EXPECTED_TURNS, so the trailing wait_turn must expect both turns (A then the queued B) before declaring ready.
kiro may either fold the queued message into the current turn or consume it as a new turn after turn A's boundary; the spec accommodates both â€” the phase contract does not assume one shape.
No model/token/cost in the kiro JSONL (sidecar-only, unread in v1) â€” observations assert model unknown, cost/tokens zero; not a daemon downgrade for this lifecycle-only scenario.

sources.note: Embedded changelog: 'Added UX for managing queued messages in V2 TUI' and 'Reject queued ! shell-escape commands during processing instead of sending them as literal text' â€” confirms the V2 TUI queues mid-turn messages (only the ! shell-escape is rejected), so agent_supports=yes.

Lines 40-83: Promptâ†’user_message; AssistantMessage with toolUseâ†’assistant (keeps working); text-only AssistantMessageâ†’turn_done; ToolResultsâ†’tool_result. State is transcript-shape driven â€” a contiguous working span with no premature turn_done.

Lines 148-161: the multi-tool turn sequence (Promptâ†’AM(toolUse)â†’ToolResultsâ†’AM(toolUse)â†’ToolResultsâ†’AM(text-only)) asserts EventType progression ending in a single turn_done â€” confirms the working span stays open across tool cycles.

Â§4-5: JSONL appends turn-by-turn; turn-end is the final text-only AssistantMessage; no model/usage/cost in the JSONL (sidecar only). Grounds the daemon emission model and the empty observation vector.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"; send_text uses tmux send-keys -l + Enter â€” the recipe's send/wait_turn/sleep are all elicited, no keys gap, driver_capability=ready.

Canonical scenario: state stays working through the queued-message arrival, no â†’ready flicker, cycle ends at ready; the irrlicht primitive is that the transcript does NOT emit a turn boundary when the queued message arrives.

preconditions: kiro-cli 2.5.1 on PATH and authenticated (subscription auth; KIRO_API_KEY not required)
irrlichd is recording (--record / grant-all) and tailing ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/
Launched as `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` so neither prompt trips a per-tool approval picker
setup: Driver launches the TUI under tmux, waits for the idle input line, and discovers the fresh <uuid>.jsonl as the newest session file after the launch MARKER
The first prompt must keep kiro busy long enough to type a follow-up into the TUI â€” a slow count-to-10 with explicit pauses is sufficient
The mid-turn follow-up is a single `send` (tmux send-keys -l + Enter) with NO preceding wait_turn, so it lands while turn A is still working; kiro's V2 TUI queues it until the next safe point

script.text: Count from 1 to 10, one number per line, with a brief pause between each. Then on the last line reply with exactly the word: done

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID entering ready, then a readyâ†’working transition for the first prompt
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready between the first readyâ†’working and the queued-message consumption (the working span is contiguous through the queued arrival)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting at any point during the working span
events.jsonl eventually shows a workingâ†’ready transition; final session state is ready

transcript.jsonl contains at least 1 Prompt line for the initial prompt; the queued follow-up appears as a second Prompt line consumed after turn A's turn_done, or is folded in â€” either shape is acceptable per spec
